notes: Night, sterile scene, 536 s. A walker goes out 21 -> ~350-370 m and returns; an outbound car reaches 403 m. Recorded on the ghostfix tracker (03e12fd).
All times are seconds from the first frame of the fixture.

Walker ground truth from a chain-scan of the RAW points (not tracker output): outbound 248->300 m t=173-206 and 292->347 m t=230-268 (dop +1.4..+1.8, az -2..+2), with a slow bridge 206-230; return trail picked up at 308 m t=346 down to 240 m t=387 (dop -1.4..-1.8). The deployed tracker held him only to 249 m (t=173) and re-acquired at 242 m (t=388).

gaps: t=268-346 the walker is beyond ~350 m / turning around and his trail is NOT chain-provable in the raw points - that window is deliberately NOT a truth segment (coverage-exempt). Do not add a segment there: no detector can honestly cover it.

The walker continues walking in after t=387 (240 -> ~21 m by ~t=519); that leg is deliberately NOT in truth either - the late-recording corridor grows a dense junk-mover carpet and the leg was not chain-scanned. Emissions there count toward the (upper-bound) ghost figure only.

The car is encoded as a STATIC BOX, not a mover: it is real (do not count its emissions as ghosts) but its journey is multi-phase (fast leave ~14 m/s, slow far leg to 403 m t=259-274) and a linear mover segment would mis-gate both binaries.
truth_complete=false: ghost counts are upper bounds.

gaps.why: beyond ~350 m / turnaround; trail not chain-provable in raw points
